gameContext.title: Terminal 1996
gameContext.mood: Paranoid, oppressive, 1990s retro-terminal aesthetic. CRT effects, screen flicker, ambient dread.
gameContext.setting: Brazil, January 1996. The player accesses a classified military terminal connected to the Varginha UFO incident — a real-life event involving alleged extraterrestrial contact, military cover-up, and witness intimidation. The game blends real historical events with cosmic horror fiction.

winCondition.description: Collect 10 evidence files by opening files marked as evidence throughout the filesystem. Once all 10 are gathered, use the "leak" command to transmit the evidence package and expose the truth.

loseConditions.description: The system traces the player's connection and terminates the session. Detection increases from opening files, failed commands, override attempts, and other suspicious actions.
Accumulating 8 wrong command attempts or authentication failures triggers permanent lockout.
Entering the wrong password for "override protocol <CODE>" three times triggers immediate security countermeasures and session termination.
Triggered by the "Terrible Mistake" — a 35% chance event when using override protocol at high detection (70%+) with 2+ evidence. Grants admin access but starts an 8-command countdown to forced purge.
Sending 8 commands that exceed the maximum input length of 256 characters triggers lockdown.

characters.name: The Kid
characters.description: A young hacker in 1996 Brazil who breaks into a classified military terminal to find evidence of the Varginha incident cover-up. Represented by a pixel-art avatar whose expression changes based on game events (neutral, shocked, scared, angry, smirk).

characters.name: UFO74
characters.role: Handler / ally embedded in the system
characters.description: A mysterious entity already inside the system who guides the player through cryptic messages. UFO74 provides reactions when evidence is found, hints when the player is stuck, and warnings about danger. Communicates in lowercase without punctuation. Has a hidden identity revealed in /sys/ghost_in_machine.enc. Appears as incognito popup messages and inline terminal text prefixed with [UFO74].

characters.name: The Firewall
characters.description: The system's automated security intelligence. Manifests as glowing eyes at screen edges (FirewallEyes component) that appear when detection >= 25% and multiply as detection rises. Initiates Turing Test evaluation at 50% detection, and triggers Firewall Lockdown at 5 evidence pieces. Not sentient but adaptive — its "personality" degrades from bureaucratic to hostile as the player digs deeper.

characters.name: Prisoner 45
characters.description: A former military sergeant from the recovery unit, now captive in an otherworldly prison. Accessible via the "chat" command. Provides increasingly disturbing testimony about the Varginha incident, alien biology, and the coming 2026 "harvest window". Has tiered responses (guarded → open → terrified) across topic categories. Gives the override password (COLHEITA) in morse code. Limited to 5 questions per session.

characters.name: Scout
characters.description: An alien entity captured during the Varginha incident, accessible through the "link" command after finding neural_dump_alfa.psi. Communicates in fragmented, elliptical phrases. Reveals the cosmic horror truth: humanity is a cognitive resource being cultivated for "harvest" by ancient watchers. Refers to itself as "we" — part of a collective consciousness. The neural link requires authentication with a conceptual phrase.

gameContext.narrativeArc: The player begins exploring what appears to be a routine classified terminal, gradually uncovering layers of the 1996 Varginha UFO incident cover-up. Starting with mundane administrative files, the investigation escalates through witness intimidation records, autopsy reports, and alien containment logs. The mid-game Firewall Lockdown at 5 evidence creates a crisis point. Post-lockdown, the override protocol (password: COLHEITA/Harvest) unlocks admin-level files revealing the cosmic horror truth: the incidents are part of a 30-year cycle of alien "harvest" operations targeting human consciousness. The endgame involves gathering all 10 evidence files and leaking them before the system traces and terminates the connection.

evidence.description: 10 evidence files scattered across the filesystem. Opening an evidence file for the first time increments the evidence counter. Evidence status shown in the HUD.
evidence.triggerAtFive: Firewall Lockdown — blocks file access until 3 security nodes are disabled

overrideProtocol.passwordMeaning: Portuguese for "harvest" — the codename for the alien operation
overrideProtocol.effect: Grants admin access (level 5), unlocks restricted directories (/admin, /storage, /ops/prato, /comms/psi, /sys)
overrideProtocol.terribleMistake: 35% chance at detection >= 70% with 2+ evidence: grants access but triggers 8-command doom countdown

leak.description: Final command to transmit gathered evidence. Available after collecting 10 evidence files. Triggers the endgame sequence.

riskAndDetection.sources: Opening files, failed commands, override attempts, trace commands, lockdown ticks
riskAndDetection.stealthRecovery: wait command (3 uses per run), hide command (available at 90%+ detection)

turingTest.description: The Firewall challenges the player to prove they are an authorized system process. A video (turing_test.mp4) plays as pre-trigger before the popup evaluation appears.

firewallLockdown.description: File access is revoked. Player must use "scan" to view 3 security nodes and "disable <node> <answer>" to shut them down. Each command during lockdown increases detection by 2%.

nodes.question: In which Brazilian city did the 1996 incident occur?
What year did the contact event take place?
What is the code name of the witness who guides you?

firewallEyes.description: Glowing amber eyes appear at screen edges when detection >= 25%. Number scales with detection: 2 eyes at 25%, up to 10 at 90%+. Purely ambient/visual — they represent The Firewall watching.

neuralLink.prerequisite: Must read neural_dump_alfa.psi to unlock. Requires conceptual passphrase for authentication.
neuralLink.description: Direct neural connection to Scout (alien consciousness). Provides fragmented alien perspective on identity, purpose, earth, future, harvest, fear, time, pain, love. Limited uses per session.

DATE: 20-JAN-1996 (07:30)

SPECIMEN FUNCTION AND PURPOSE ANALYSIS
CLASSIFICATION: RESTRICTED — 22-FEB-1996

CONCLUSION: The recovered entities are tools, not envoys.

Anatomical evidence shows deliberate engineering:
sensory organs optimized for observation, neural density
exceeding body requirements, simplified digestive system.
Poorly suited for Earth gravity. No immune response.
No reproductive capability. No autonomous decision centers.

These are reconnaissance units. Purpose-built. Never meant
to survive, return, or represent their creators. Biological
sensors. Nothing more.

IMPLICATION: If Earth received scouts, it passed screening.
If scouts transmitted, data was received. If data was
received, we have been catalogued. Something now knows
we are here, what we are, and what we are worth.

RE: Decentralized Currency Prototype — Phase II Assessment

Per your request, we have completed preliminary testing of
the distributed ledger monetary system ("Project COIN").

KEY FINDINGS:

  1. The cryptographic consensus mechanism functions as
     designed. Nodes achieve agreement without central
     authority within acceptable latency parameters.

     requirements for untraceable fund transfers.

  3. Energy consumption remains a concern. Current mining
     algorithms require significant computational resources.

  Continue research but delay public deployment. The
  technology is premature for civilian adoption but shows
  promise for covert operations funding channels.

  Suggest revisiting for public release in 10-15 years
  when infrastructure can support wider adoption.

DOCUMENT: PA-1969-07 (DECLASSIFIED EXCERPT)
ORIGINAL DATE: 14-JUL-1969
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

SUBJECT: Handling Visual Inconsistencies in Mission Footage

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

BACKGROUND:

  During post-production review of lunar surface footage,
  technical staff identified several lighting anomalies
  that may generate public confusion.

IDENTIFIED ISSUES:

  - Shadow direction variance in Frames 1247-1289
  - Multiple apparent light sources in EVA footage
  - Crosshair positioning behind foreground objects
  - Flag movement without atmospheric conditions

GUIDANCE:

  1. Do NOT proactively address these inconsistencies.

  2. If questioned, attribute anomalies to:
     a) Camera lens artifacts
     b) Reflected light from lunar surface
     c) Electromagnetic interference with equipment

  3. Under NO circumstances acknowledge that backup
     footage was prepared at [REDACTED] facility.

  4. Emphasize mission success narrative over technical
     details of visual documentation.

NOTE: This guidance supersedes previous directives.

APPROVED BY: DIR. COMMUNICATIONS

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CONSUMER BEHAVIOR RESEARCH — ACOUSTIC INFLUENCE STUDY
PROJECT: TEMPO
DATE: Q4 1995 FINAL REPORT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

STUDY OVERVIEW:

  In partnership with [REDACTED] retail chains, this study
  evaluated the effect of ambient audio parameters on
  consumer behavior patterns.

METHODOLOGY:

  - 847 retail locations participated
  - Music tempo varied from 60-120 BPM across test groups
  - Subliminal audio tones embedded at 17.5 Hz
  - Control group received standard muzak programming

FINDINGS:

  1. TEMPO CORRELATION
     - 72 BPM: 18% increase in browsing duration
     - 108 BPM: 23% increase in purchase velocity
     - 60 BPM: Measurable increase in high-margin purchases

  2. SUBLIMINAL TONE EFFECTS
     - 17.5 Hz baseline: Elevated stress markers
     - 12 Hz modification: Reduced price sensitivity
     - Combined with verbal suggestions: Inconclusive

  3. OPTIMAL CONFIGURATION
     - Morning: 108 BPM (urgency)
     - Afternoon: 72 BPM (extended browsing)
     - Pre-closing: 120 BPM (clear out)

RECOMMENDATION:

  Implement Phase II testing in food service environments.
  Preliminary data suggests eating pace modification
